title: One concept of agent (uniformity)
statement: An agent is a single uniform concept — a named callable unit with typed inputs, typed outputs, context-parametric implementation, and capability-granted tool execution when declared. There is no distinction between monitor agent / workflow agent / privileged agent at the framework level. The same spec format, compilation pipeline, dispatch runtime, and output-validation contract apply to every agent regardless of consumer.
rationale: Uniformity is the framework correctness criterion. Special-casing per consumer multiplies code paths and contradicts P3 (recreatability via framework alone).
